structure.text:
Subject: NCM-P-2025-08-25 Loan Assumption Process Change
From: Nova Capital Markets <capitalmarkets@novahomeloans.com>
Date: 2025-08-25 11:21:32-07:00

 



ANNOUNCEMENT SUMMARY

As you are aware, NOVA is transitioning to a new sub-servicer named ServBank.  Unlike ServiceMac, ServBank does not process assumptions and therefore NOVA is building out a process to handle these requests.

 

PROCESS OVERVIEW

If you are asked or become aware that a buyer or borrower wants to explore an assumption, please have them send an email to loanservicing@novahomeloans.com <mailto:loanservicing@novahomeloans.com>  to start the process.  The email should contain the requestor’s name, contact information, subject property address, and seller’s name (if applicable).  Loan Servicing will log the request and then forward it to Isabel Bustamante.  Isabel will contact the buyer/borrower and provide them with a needs list and answer any questions.  Once the requestor has provided the required documentation, we will order a credit report, preliminary title report and any other required third-party item.  We will then disclose the costs that the requestor will pay, including the delta between the sales price and current unpaid principal balance.  The person assuming the existing loan will also be responsible for fees related to credit reports, title and escrow, pro-rations, new HOI, any HOA transfers, etc.

 

FEES AND QUALIFICATION

Lenders are allowed to charge fees for assumptions:  

 

*	FHA - $1,800.00
*	VA - $250.00 + a .5% funding fee, payable to VA
*	Conventional – 1.0% of the current loan balance  

 

The requestor must qualify to assume the loan just as they would if they were getting a new loan.  All guidelines, LTV, CLTV, DTI, etc., apply.  If the requestor does not qualify, the assumption will be denied.  Upon approval, the requestor will attend a closing with the escrow officer of their choice (or as determined by the sales contract).  Conventional loans are only assumable in cases of divorce, inheritance, or for loss mitigation purposes.

 

If secondary financing is required, there are guidelines to be followed:

 

*	FHA – second lien must have level (fixed) payments so a HELOC would not be allowed, but a closed-end second would be acceptable.
*	VA – a HELOC or closed-end second are allowed.
*	Conventional – a HELOC or closed-end second are allowed.

 

IMPORTANT NOTES

*	Borrowers who have already reached out will receive the needs list shortly
*	As additional information is available, announcements will be issued.  If you have any questions, please contact Jana Gledhill or Isabel Bustamante.

 

** For NOVA Home Loans Internal Distribution Only **

For inquiries, contact:

 

Loan Servicing <mailto:loanservicing@novahomeloans.com>         Jana Gledhill <mailto:jana.gledhill@novahomeloans.com>         Isabel Bustamante <mailto:isabel.bustamante@novahomeloans.com>
